title: ZLAR Governed Action Receipt v1 (Envelope)
description: Portable cryptographic proof of a governed action. Envelope format: payload is base64url-encoded canonical JSON, signature covers those exact bytes. Verifiers never re-canonicalize — they hash the decoded payload and verify the signature. Anyone with the public key can verify.

v.description: Receipt format version. Determines the entire cryptographic construction: v1 = Ed25519 + SHA-256 + ZLAR canonical JSON payload. No algorithm negotiation — the version IS the algorithm. Verifiers MUST reject unknown versions.

id.description: Unique receipt identifier. Hex timestamp (12 chars) + random bytes (32 chars).

kid.description: Key identifier. First 16 characters of SHA-256 hash of the signing public key file. Enables efficient key selection when multiple keys exist.

iat.description: Issued-at timestamp as Unix epoch seconds (integer, not string). When the receipt was created.

type.description: Receipt type. v1 supports only 'governed-action'. Future versions may add types.

payload.description: Base64url-encoded (no padding) UTF-8 bytes of the canonical JSON payload. The payload contains all governance fields. Canonicalization follows the ZLAR Canonicalization Specification v1.0 (docs/canonicalization-spec.md). The verifier decodes this to get the raw bytes for hash verification, then parses the JSON to read the governance fields.

sig.description: Base64url-encoded (no padding) Ed25519 signature. Signing: SHA-256 hash the decoded payload bytes → hex string → Ed25519 sign the hex string bytes. Verification: reverse the process.

prev.description: SHA-256 hash of the previous receipt (canonical envelope JSON), or null for the first receipt. Links receipts into a chain.
